zh.lifeSummary: 當Leopold I., der Glorwürdige bzw. Das Schwert Habsburg, Herzog von Österreich und der Steiermark於1290年8月4日出生在奧地利維恩時，他的父親Albrecht II von Habsburg35歲，母親Elisabeth of Carinthia27歲。他於1315年與Catherine de SAVOIE結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1326年2月28日在法國阿爾薩斯巴-爾斯特拉斯布格享年35歲，埋葬在瑞士阿高文迪可特格斯費爾登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Leopold I., der Glorwürdige bzw. Das Schwert Habsburg, Herzog von Österreich und der Steiermark于1290年8月4日出生在奥地利維恩时，他的父亲Albrecht II von Habsburg35岁，母亲Elisabeth of Carinthia27岁。他于1315年与Catherine de SAVOIE结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。他去世于1326年2月28日在法国阿爾薩斯巴-爾斯特拉斯布格享年35岁，埋葬在瑞士阿高文迪可特格斯費爾登。

nameMeanings.name: Herzog
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Erwin, Fritz, Heinz, Kurt, Otto, Wolfgang, Franz, Arno, Ernst, Hildegarde, Reinhold.

German: from the Middle High German title of nobility herzoge ‘duke’ (Old High German herizoho, from heri ‘army’ + ziohan ‘to lead’, a calque of the Byzantine title stratēlatēs ‘general, commander’, from Greek stratos ‘army’ + elaunein ‘to lead’). The name is unlikely to refer to descent from an actual duke; it is normally an occupational name for the servant of a duke or a nickname for one who put on the airs and graces of a duke. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, and Slovenia (see also 3 below). Compare Hertzog .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Herzog ‘duke’ (see 1 above). Compare Graf and Kaiser .

variants.name: Hartsook
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herceg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Croatian Stanko.

Croatian: nickname from herceg ‘duke’, a title of the medieval feudal lords of Herzegovina (‘duke's land’, a region in Bosnia and Herzegovina), derived from the Hungarian form of German Herzog . Compare Erceg .

variants.name: Herczeg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Imre, Laszlo, Tibor, Attila, Ferenc, Gaza, Istvan, Janos.
Hungarian form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herzig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Fritzi, Hans, Horst, Markus, Monika.
German (eastern and central Germany and Silesia, Switzerland): variant of   Herzog  .
German: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names composed with hard ‘strong, hardy’, for instance Herto.

variants.name: Hertzog
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Herzog and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Hertzog is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

variants.name: Hartzog
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog   1.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsock
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsoe
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsough
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Österreich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Kurt, Uwe, Wolfgang.

German (rarely Österreich) and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from the province of Upper or Lower Austria, from German Österreich ‘Austria’, the name of which literally means ‘Eastern kingdom’.

variants.name: Oestreicher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Franz, Otto, Horst.  Jewish Shloime, Chaim, Mendy.
German (also Östreicher) and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from the province of Upper or Lower Austria (see Oesterreich ).

variants.name: Oestreich
meanings: German (also Östreich) and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Oesterreich .

nameMeanings.name: und
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Myer.

Jewish (Ashkenazic): from German Pfund ‘pound’, either a nickname or one of the names chosen at random from vocabulary words in the 18th and 19th centuries by government officials at the time when surnames became compulsory.
Altered form of German   Pfund  .

variants.name: Pfund
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Claus, Erwin, Gerhard.

German: metonymic occupational name for a sealer of weights, or for a wholesale merchant, from Middle High German pfunt ‘pound’ (as a measure of weight and a unit of currency), or a nickname relating to a tax obligation.

nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Istvan, Janos, Lajos.
Hungarian (Dér): from the old personal name Dér of Turkic origin, or a nickname for a gray-haired man, from the vocabulary word dér ‘hoarfrost’.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 謝, see   Xie   1.

variants.name: Xie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Hong, Li, Ming, Ying, Min, Qing, Yuan, Bin, Hui, Ping, Wei, Xiaoli, Ding, Chang, Lan, Quan, Cong, Dang, Hai, Tian, Yiping.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 謝, meaning ‘thank’ in Chinese: (i) from Xie (謝), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in Nanyang in Henan province), annexed around the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).Later this place was probably granted to Shen Bo (the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After it was annexed during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Zhi-Le (直勒) by Xie Xiaozheng (謝孝政), an official during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Leopold
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from liut ‘people’ + bold ‘bold, brave’. The first element was altered by association with Latin leo ‘lion’. A name of this origin may have been introduced into Britain by the Normans, but if so it did not survive long. It was reintroduced to Britain from the Continent towards the end of the 19th‐century in honour of Leopold , King of the Belgians ( 1790–1865 ) and uncle of Queen Victoria , to whom he was an influential adviser in her youth: she named one of her sons after him.

nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Dev .

nameMeanings.name: bzw.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: habitational name from any of various minor places called with Old English boga ‘bow, arch, vault’, i.e. an arched bridge. There are places so called in Midlothian, Middlesex, and Devon.
Irish and English: variant of   Bowes  .

Chinese: possibly from Cantonese form of the Chinese names 寶 (meaning ‘treasure’) or 保 (meaning ‘protect’), which were monosyllabic personal names or part of disyllabic personal names of some early Chinese immigrants in the US.

variants.name: Bowe
meanings: English and Irish: variant (mainly Irish) of   Bow  .

variants.name: Bowes
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from Bowes (formerly in North Yorkshire, now in County Durham), or from some other place so called, the placename being derived from the plural of Old English boga ‘bow’, here referring to bends in a river. Alternatively, a topographic name for someone who lived ‘(at the) arches or bridge’. See Bow 1.
English: variant of Bow , with excrescent -s.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Buadhaigh (see Bogue ).

variants.name: Bough
meanings: Irish: Possibly derived from beać, a bee

nameMeanings.name: Das
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse dagr ‘day’.

nameMeanings.name: Schwert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Swiss German: probably an endearing form of a relationship name meaning ‘father-in-law, brother-in-law’, Middle High German sweher, swēr (see Schwer ).

variants.name: Schwer
meanings: South German: relationship name from Middle High German sweher, swēr ‘father-in-law’. Compare Schwager and Schwehr .
German: variant of   Schweder   2.
German: nickname from Middle High German swær ‘painful, unpleasant, troublesome; sad, sorrowful; weighty, heavy’.

historicEvents.description: Three valleys in Central Switzerland unite against the counts of Habsburg and fight for autonomy. Cities join the confederacy. They conquer territories in northern and southern Switzerland.
Decisive Battle against the counts of Habsburg.

en.lifeSummary: When Leopold I., der Glorwürdige bzw. Das Schwert Habsburg, Herzog von Österreich und der Steiermark was born on 4 August 1290, in Vienna, Austria, his father, Albrecht II von Habsburg, was 35 and his mother, Elisabeth of Carinthia, was 27. He married Catherine de SAVOIE in 1315. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died on 28 February 1326, in Strasbourg, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, France, at the age of 35, and was buried in Königsfelden, Windisch, Aargau, Switzerland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Leopold I., der Glorwürdige bzw. Das Schwert Habsburg, Herzog von Österreich und der Steiermark est né le 4 août 1290, à Vienne, Autriche, son père, Albrecht II von Habsburg, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Elisabeth of Carinthia, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Catherine de SAVOIE en 1315. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé le 28 février 1326, à Strasbourg, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, France, à l’âge de 35 ans, et a été enterré à Königsfelden, Windisch, Argovie, Suisse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Leopold I., der Glorwürdige bzw. Das Schwert Habsburg, Herzog von Österreich und der Steiermark am 4. August 1290 in Wien, Österreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Albrecht II von Habsburg 35 und seine Mutter Elisabeth of Carinthia 27. Er hat 1315 Catherine de SAVOIE geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb am 28. Februar 1326 im Alter von 35 Jahren in Straßburg, Niederrhein, Elsaß, Frankreich und wurde in Königsfelden, Windisch, Aargau, Schweiz begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Leopold I., der Glorwürdige bzw. Das Schwert Habsburg, Herzog von Österreich und der Steiermark è nato il il 4 agosto 1290 nel Wien, Austria, il suo padre, Albrecht II von Habsburg, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Elisabeth of Carinthia, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Catherine de SAVOIE nel 1315. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 28 febbraio 1326, nel Strasbourg, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, Francia, all'età di 35 anni, e fu sepolto a Königsfelden, Windisch, Argovia, Svizzera.

ja.lifeSummary: Leopold I., der Glorwürdige bzw. Das Schwert Habsburg, Herzog von Österreich und der Steiermarkが1290年8月4日にオーストリアウィンで生まれた時，父Albrecht II von Habsburgは35歳，母Elisabeth of Carinthiaは27歳であった。1315年にCatherine de SAVOIEと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は1326年2月28日にフランスアルサスバス-ルヒンストラスボウルグで35歳で死去し，スイスアールガウウィンディッシュクoニグスフエルデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Leopold I., der Glorwürdige bzw. Das Schwert Habsburg, Herzog von Österreich und der Steiermark(이)가 1290년 8월 4일 오스트리아 빈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Albrecht II von Habsburg(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Elisabeth of Carinthia(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1315년 Catherine de SAVOIE(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1326년 2월 28일 프랑스 알사스 바스-히 스트라스보우르그에서 35세의 나이로 사망했으며 스위스 아아르가 윈디슈 ko니그스펠든에 묻혔다.

mn.4. August 1290–28. Februar 1326: 1290 оны Наймдугаар сарын 4–1326 оны Хоёрдугаар сарын 28

pt.4. August 1290–28. Februar 1326: 4 de agosto de 1290–28 de fevereiro de 1326
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Leopold I., der Glorwürdige bzw. Das Schwert Habsburg, Herzog von Österreich und der Steiermark nasceu em 4 de agosto de 1290, em Wien, Áustria, seu pai, Albrecht II von Habsburg, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Elisabeth of Carinthia, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Catherine de SAVOIE em 1315. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 28 de fevereiro de 1326, em Strasbourg, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, França, com 35 anos, e foi sepultado em Königsfelden, Windisch, Aargau, Suiça.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Leopold I., der Glorwürdige bzw. Das Schwert Habsburg, Herzog von Österreich und der Steiermark родился 4 августа 1290 г. в местности Вена, Австрия, его отцу по имени Albrecht II von Habsburg было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Elisabeth of Carinthia, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine de SAVOIE в 1315 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер 28 февраля 1326 г. в местности страсбург, бас-рин, алсеис, Франция в возрасте 35 лет и был похоронен в местности кoнигсфелден, виндищ, ааргау, Швейцария.

es.4. August 1290–28. Februar 1326: 4 de agosto de 1290–28 de febrero de 1326
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Leopold I., der Glorwürdige bzw. Das Schwert Habsburg, Herzog von Österreich und der Steiermark nació el 4 de agosto de 1290, en Wien, Austria, su papá, Albrecht II von Habsburg, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Elisabeth of Carinthia, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Catherine de SAVOIE en 1315. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió el 28 de febrero de 1326, en Strasbourg, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, Francia, a la edad de 35 años, y fue sepultado en Königsfelden, Windisch, Aargau, Suiza.
